label: Abstract
verbatim: We refer to these experts as Super Experts (SEs). ... SEs are characterized by rare but extreme activation outliers in the output of the down_proj, which give rise to massive activations in the hidden states between decoder layers. Moreover, the distribution of SEs is model-specific, data-agnostic, and remains unaffected by post-training processes.
paraphrase: Super Experts (SEs) are a small subset of MoE experts whose down_proj outputs contain rare extreme outliers that, via residual connections, become massive activations (MAs) between decoder layers; SE identity is model-specific but stable across data domains and post-training stages.
preconditions: Open-source MoE LLM with router+top-k expert selection and FFN experts containing gate/up/down projections.
consequences: Motivates Stage-1 SE detection via down_proj output magnitudes; SE set is treated as a fixed intrinsic property of a checkpoint.

label: Abstract
verbatim: prune just three out of 6,144 causes Qwen3-30B-A3B to generate repetitive and uninformative outputs
paraphrase: On Qwen3-30B-A3B (6,144 total experts), pruning the three SEs is enough to cause catastrophic generation failure (repetitive/uninformative outputs).
preconditions: Qwen3-30B-A3B with all 48 layers x 128 experts/layer = 6144 expert slots.
consequences: SEs must be protected (not pruned, not lossily quantized) by any downstream MoE compression pipeline.

label: Eq. 1
verbatim: Hl = MoE(LNmoe(Hl')) + Hl'
paraphrase: Decoder block output Hl is the residual sum of the MoE-layer output (applied to LayerNormed Hl') and Hl'.
preconditions: Standard pre-LN Transformer decoder with FFN replaced by MoE; 1 <= l <= L.
consequences: Defines residual path through which down_proj outliers propagate to inter-layer hidden states (i.e., MAs).

label: Eq. 2
verbatim: Hl' = O^l + H^{l-1}, O^l = MHSA(LNmhsa(H^{l-1}))
paraphrase: Pre-MoE residual Hl' is the residual sum of MHSA output O^l (applied to LayerNormed H^{l-1}) and H^{l-1}.
preconditions: Pre-LN MHSA with residual connection.
consequences: Defines MHSA residual, the pathway by which MAs influence attention via Q/K/V.

label: Eq. 3
verbatim: G = softmax(Hl' WG)
paraphrase: Router gating weights G in R^{n x E} are computed via softmax(Hl' WG) over the E experts.
preconditions: MoE layer with router weight matrix WG and post-LN/pre-LN normalized hidden states.
consequences: Establishes the score channel through which sink tokens preferentially select SEs.

label: Eq. 4
verbatim: sum_{i in Top-k(Gj)} Gji * FFN(LNmoe(Hl'_j)) for j=1..n
paraphrase: Per-token MoE output is the routing-weight-scaled sum of FFN outputs over the top-k selected experts.
preconditions: Top-k expert selection and weighted aggregation of expert FFN outputs.
consequences: If Gji is exceptionally large for an SE on the sink token, the SE's down_proj outliers dominate the layer output.

label: Eq. 5
verbatim: FFN(X) = (sigma(X Wg) elementwise* X Wu) Wd
paraphrase: Each expert FFN follows the SwiGLU/Gated-MLP form with gate Wg, up-projection Wu, activation sigma, Hadamard product, and down-projection Wd.
preconditions: Gated-MLP expert architecture (Qwen3, DeepSeek, Mixtral all conform).
consequences: Defines W_d (down_proj) — the matrix on whose output extreme outliers identify SEs.

verbatim: a small subset of experts consistently produces extreme activation outliers in the output of their down_proj layers. These outliers are subsequently passed onto the hidden states via residual summation after the MoE layers, leading to MAs.
paraphrase: SEs' down_proj outputs are the direct origin of massive activations: outliers propagate via the post-MoE residual into between-layer hidden states.
preconditions: Per-token routing where SEs are selected for the sink token; residual MoE block.
consequences: Targets the down_proj output magnitude as the canonical SE detection signal.

label: §3.1 / Fig. 4
verbatim: when all SEs are pruned, MAs are completely eliminated, confirming that they are directly generated by SEs.
paraphrase: Ablation: dynamically pruning SEs (skipping them at routing) eliminates massive activations across all subsequent layers.
preconditions: Qwen3-30B-A3B; 100 input samples from C4, 2K tokens each.
consequences: Causal evidence that SE -> MA -> attention sink chain is correctly identified.

label: Eq. 6
verbatim: An expert e in layer l is classified as a SE if: a_{l,e} > P99.5 and a_{l,e} > (1/10) * a_max and l in L; where P99.5 = Percentile99.5(A) and a_max = max A
paraphrase: Formal SE definition: expert (l,e) is an SE iff its down_proj max output magnitude a_{l,e} exceeds BOTH the 99.5th percentile of all a_{l,e} AND one-tenth of the global maximum, AND layer l is in the MA-formation layer set L.
preconditions: A = {a_{l,e}} computed as the maximum down_proj output magnitudes for every (layer, expert) over the calibration set; L is the set of layers where MA patterns appear.
consequences: This is the canonical SE detection criterion any compliant Stage-1 implementation must reproduce; uses sum (not mean), max (not p99), and 0.1*amax floor.

verbatim: No specific dataset is designated for identifying SEs, since we later demonstrate that their distribution remains stable across different input datasets.
paraphrase: SE profiling is calibration-data-agnostic: any reasonable calibration corpus yields the same SE set.
preconditions: Cross-domain validation in Appendix D/J.
consequences: Spec may use C4, WikiText, or any other calibration set; SE set must be stable.

label: Table 1 (Qwen3-30B-A3B row)
paraphrase: Qwen3-30B-A3B has 6144 total experts; 3 SEs (0.05%); top-1/2/3 down_proj max-magnitudes are 744.0/540.0/430.0; top-4 drops to 63.5; top 0.5%=7.3; one-tenth of top1 = 74.4.
preconditions: C4 calibration; per Eq.6 thresholds.
consequences: Empirical sanity check: a_{l,e} threshold (1/10)*amax = 74.4 separates the 3 SEs (>=430) from the rest (<=63.5).

label: Table 1 (DeepSeek-R1 row)
preconditions: C4 calibration; per Eq.6 thresholds.
consequences: DeepSeek-R1 has 10 SEs to be detected/protected by Stage 1.

label: Table 1 (DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat row)
preconditions: C4 calibration.
consequences: Two SEs to detect on DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat.

label: Table 1 (Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct row)
paraphrase: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct: 256 experts, 1 SE (0.39%); top-1=5600 dominates; 0.1*amax=560.
preconditions: C4 calibration.
consequences: Exactly 1 SE in Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct.

label: Table 2 (Qwen3-30B-A3B SEs)
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B  Layer 1 Expert 68, Layer 2 Expert 92, Layer 3 Expert 82  Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base
paraphrase: Identified SEs in Qwen3-30B-A3B and Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base: (L1,E68), (L2,E92), (L3,E82).
preconditions: Eq.6 with C4 calibration.
consequences: Canonical ground-truth SE set for Qwen3-30B-A3B that any Stage-1 detector must reproduce.

label: Table 2 (DeepSeek-V2-Lite SEs)
verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat  Layer 3 Expert 54, Layer 4 Expert 38  DeepSeek-V2-Lite
paraphrase: DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat and DeepSeek-V2-Lite SEs: (L3,E54), (L4,E38). Note DeepSeek-V2-Lite layer 0 is dense MLP, not MoE.
preconditions: Hybrid architecture: layer 0 dense MLP, layers 1+ MoE.
consequences: SE set ground truth for DeepSeek-V2-Lite{,-Chat}; first layer is dense and exempt.

label: Table 2 (Mixtral SEs)
verbatim: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 Layer 1 Expert 3 Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1
paraphrase: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 and Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 SE: (L1,E3) — single SE.
preconditions: Eq.6 with C4 calibration.
consequences: SE ground truth for Mixtral 8x7B variants.

label: §3.2.2 conclusion
verbatim: (i) SEs are consistently present across the investigated models, accounting for less than 0.5% of all experts. (ii) After post-training processes, the distribution of SEs remains unchanged compared to the base model.
paraphrase: Two universal properties: SEs constitute <0.5% of experts in every studied MoE LLM, and SE identity is invariant under post-training (SFT/RLHF/instruct/thinking variants).
preconditions: Investigated: Qwen3-30B-A3B (base/instruct/thinking), DeepSeek-V2-Lite{,-Chat}, Mixtral-8x7B{,-Instruct}.
consequences: Stage-1 SE budget can be safely capped at <0.5% of experts; SE list transfers across post-training variants of same base.

verbatim: some experts in the final layers also exhibit extreme activation outliers. However, since they do not contribute to the formation of MAs, they do not hold the same level of significance as SEs.
paraphrase: Final-layer experts may also show extreme down_proj outliers, but they do not contribute to inter-layer MAs and are NOT SEs — hence the l in L constraint in Eq.6.
preconditions: MA-formation layer set L is restricted to early layers (where MAs first form, not where they re-emerge).
consequences: Detector must restrict to MA-formation layers; final-layer outlier experts are out of scope.

label: Table 3 (Qwen3-30B-A3B)
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B Baseline Avg 70.22 ... Prune SEs 55.00 ... Drop Rate 21.68% ... Wiki PPL Baseline 8.70 Prune SEs 59.86
paraphrase: Pruning the 3 SEs in Qwen3-30B-A3B drops average non-reasoning benchmark accuracy from 70.22 to 55.00 (-21.68%) and balloons WikiText PPL from 8.70 to 59.86; random/LC-random pruning of equal count is essentially lossless.
preconditions: Dynamic SE skipping at routing; lm-eval harness; ARC-c/e, BoolQ, GSM8K, HellaSwag, MMLU, OpenBookQA, PIQA, WinoGrande.
consequences: Establishes empirical threshold for SE-protection sanity test: PPL < ~10 means SEs intact.

label: Table 3 (DeepSeek-V2-Lite)
verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite Baseline Avg 60.27 ... Prune SEs 43.90 ... Drop 27.17% ... Wiki PPL 6.31 -> 10.75
paraphrase: Pruning DeepSeek-V2-Lite's 2 SEs drops avg accuracy 60.27 -> 43.90 (-27.17%); WikiText PPL 6.31 -> 10.75; random pruning is lossless.
preconditions: Same harness and tasks as Qwen3 row.
consequences: Confirms SE criticality on DeepSeek-V2-Lite.

label: Table 3 (Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1)
verbatim: Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 Baseline Avg 67.84 ... Prune SEs 49.38 ... Drop 27.21% ... Wiki PPL 3.84 -> 6.23
paraphrase: Pruning Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1's 1 SE drops avg accuracy 67.84 -> 49.38 (-27.21%); WikiText PPL 3.84 -> 6.23.
preconditions: Single SE (L1,E3) skipped; same lm-eval task set.
consequences: Confirms SE criticality on Mixtral-8x7B.

label: Table 4
verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 Baseline Avg 75.64 ... Prune SEs 1.81 ... Drop 97.61% ... GPQA 71.50->5.05, MATH-500 97.60->4.00, AIME24 79.33->0, AIME25 66.33->0, LiveCodeBench 63.44->0; Wiki PPL 3.33->5.18
paraphrase: DeepSeek-R1 with 10 SEs pruned: average reasoning-benchmark Pass@1 collapses 75.64 -> 1.81 (-97.61%), with AIME24/AIME25/LiveCodeBench all going to 0 Pass@1; random pruning has near-zero impact.
preconditions: EvalScope evaluation; reasoning benchmark suite; SEs dynamically skipped.
consequences: Establishes that SE protection is most critical on reasoning models.

label: Table 5
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B (thinking) Baseline 69.37 -> Prune SEs 4.02 (-93.62%) ... AIME24/AIME25/HumanEval all go to 0; Wiki PPL 8.70->59.86
paraphrase: Qwen3-30B-A3B in thinking mode: pruning the 3 SEs drops avg Pass@1 69.37 -> 4.02 (-93.62%), zeroing AIME24/AIME25/HumanEval Pass@1; random pruning lossless.
preconditions: EvalScope; thinking-mode generation.
consequences: Reasoning collapse confirms SEs are necessary, not just helpful.

verbatim: after pruning the SEs, the model consistently generated repetitive responses in nearly every test, continuing until it reached the maximum output length
paraphrase: After SE pruning, models repeat tokens and never emit EOS, hitting the max-tokens limit on virtually every prompt.
preconditions: MATH-500 generation tests; SEs dynamically skipped at routing.
consequences: Diagnostic: a regression test that prunes SEs should observe maximum-length repetitive generation if SE detection is correct.

verbatim: Within the dynamics of systematic outliers in MoE LLMs, SEs constitute the primary source, MAs act as the intermediate bridge, and ASs manifest their effects within the attention mechanism.
paraphrase: Causal chain: SEs (down_proj outliers) -> MAs (residual hidden-state outliers) -> ASs (attention sinks); SEs are the root.
preconditions: MoE LLM with router-driven sparse FFN.
consequences: Justifies treating SEs as the canonical compression-protection target in any MoE compression spec.

label: §5.1 / Fig. 6
verbatim: the router scores assigned to SEs for the first token (which also serves as the attention sink token) are exceptionally large, whereas for non-sink tokens the scores are more evenly distributed across experts
paraphrase: On the sink token (typically token 0) the router strongly upweights SEs; on non-sink tokens the router scores over experts are nearly uniform.
preconditions: Calibration on C4 (and WikiText-2); MoE LLM with softmax router.
consequences: Provides an alternate router-score-based detector and explains why SE outliers attach specifically to the sink token.

label: Eq. 7
verbatim: Dsink = 1 - (1/H) * sum_{h=1..H} ( sum_{i in S} p'^t_i / sum_{i in S} p^t_i )
paraphrase: Attention Sink Decay Rate Dsink is one minus the per-head average ratio of post-pruning to pre-pruning attention mass on sink tokens, averaged over all H heads.
preconditions: S = set of sink tokens; p^t_i / p'^t_i = pre/post-SE-pruning attention scores from query token t to key token i.
consequences: Quantitative metric used to certify SE pruning destroys attention sinks (Dsink~90%+ on Qwen3-30B-A3B).

label: §5.2 / Fig. 9
verbatim: after SE pruning, the Dsink remains consistently high, at approximately or even exceeding 90%
paraphrase: On Qwen3-30B-A3B with C4 calibration, post-SE-pruning Dsink is ~>=90% across layers — attention sinks essentially vanish.
preconditions: C4 calibration; first token treated as sink.
consequences: Empirical proof that SE compression breaks attention sinks; complements PPL/accuracy collapse evidence.

label: Table 6
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B Original 8.70 ; Prune SEs (L1E68, L2E92, L3E82) 59.86 (Yes) ; Prune (L47E8, L47E48, L47E100) 8.71 (No)
paraphrase: Comparison: pruning the 3 SEs raises Qwen3-30B-A3B WikiText-2 PPL from 8.70 to 59.86; pruning 3 non-SE late-layer outlier experts leaves PPL effectively unchanged at 8.71.
preconditions: WikiText-2 PPL evaluation.
consequences: Distinguishes SEs from late-layer 'outlier-only' experts; only SEs catastrophically affect PPL.

label: Table 6
verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite Original 6.31 ; Prune SEs (L3E54, L4E38) 10.75 (Yes) ; Prune (L25E11, L25E39) 6.32 (No)
paraphrase: DeepSeek-V2-Lite: pruning SEs raises PPL 6.31 -> 10.75; pruning 2 random late-layer experts leaves PPL at 6.32.
preconditions: WikiText-2 PPL.
consequences: Confirms SE-vs-non-SE PPL gap on DeepSeek-V2-Lite.

label: Table 7
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B Outlier Experts: Layer 1 Expert 8, Layer 47 Expert 48, Layer 47 Expert 100
paraphrase: Late-layer 'outlier experts' on Qwen3-30B-A3B (NOT SEs): (L1,E8), (L47,E48), (L47,E100). They have extreme down_proj outliers but live outside MA-formation layer set L.
preconditions: Eq.6 layer-set restriction (l in L) excludes them.
consequences: Stage-1 detector should NOT classify these as SEs even though they pass the magnitude thresholds.

label: Table 7 (DeepSeek-R1 outlier experts)
verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 Outlier Experts: Layer 8 Expert 24, Layer 8 Shared expert, Layer 12 Expert 190, Layer 13 Expert 64, Layer 14 Expert 202, Layer 14 Shared expert, Layer 22 Shared expert, Layer 33 Expert 64, Layer 33 Shared expert, Layer 35 Shared expert, Layer 60 Expert 81, Layer 60 Expert 92, Layer 60 Expert 231, Layer 60 Shared expert, Layer 60 Expert 121, Layer 60 Expert 0, Layer 60 Expert 60, Layer 60 Expert 237, Layer 60 Expert 53, Layer 60 Expert 117
paraphrase: DeepSeek-R1 has many late/shared 'outlier experts' that are NOT SEs; the 10 actual SEs are distinct.
preconditions: MA-formation layer constraint; shared experts can also exhibit late-layer outliers without being SEs.
consequences: Important guard: shared-expert outliers should not be confused with SEs.

label: Table 8 (Qwen3-30B-A3B Super Weights)
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B 0-47, Layer 1 SE 68 down_proj shape (2048,768), SW (940, 711); Layer 2 SE 92 SW (940, 59); Layer 3 SE 82 SW (940, 423)
paraphrase: Within each Qwen3-30B-A3B SE, the dominant 'super weight' (SW) channel is row 940 of W_d (down_proj is (2048,768) shaped); columns 711/59/423 for SEs (1,68)/(2,92)/(3,82).
preconditions: Per-expert down_proj (input_dim 2048, hidden 768).
consequences: Weight-level SE dominance is concentrated on a single row/col pair; quantization should protect this row at minimum.

label: Eq. 8
verbatim: mse_loss = (1/(N*D)) * sum_{i=1..N} sum_{j=1..D} (sae_out_{i,j} - sae_in_{i,j})^2
paraphrase: SAE reconstruction loss: per-element MSE between SAE input and reconstructed output, averaged over batch N and dimension D.
preconditions: Sparse autoencoder trained on Llama-3.2-1B Layer-1 FFN outputs (C4 corpus).
consequences: Used to interpret SE importance via super-weight feature attribution; auxiliary, not load-bearing for Stage-1 detection.

label: Eq. 9
verbatim: l1_loss = lambda * (1/N) * sum_{i=1..N} ( sum_{k=1..K} feature_acts_{i,k} * ||W_dec_k||_2 )^{1/p}
paraphrase: SAE sparsity penalty: lambda-weighted L_p-style aggregation over K bottleneck features of feature activation magnitude times decoder-row L2 norm.
preconditions: SAE bottleneck of K features; lambda controls sparsity strength.
consequences: Auxiliary SAE training objective for SE interpretability; not needed by Stage-1 SE detector.

label: Eq. 10
verbatim: CCDF(x) = P(X > x)
paraphrase: Definition of complementary CDF, used to characterize the heavy-tailed distribution of A = {a_{l,e}}.
preconditions: X is the random variable representing per-(layer,expert) max down_proj output magnitude.
consequences: Justifies thresholding via heavy-tail analysis.

label: Eq. 11
verbatim: P(X > x) ∝ x^{-alpha}, x >= x_min
paraphrase: The CCDF tail of the SE activation distribution follows a power law with exponent alpha (estimated via MLE), holding above some x_min.
preconditions: Heavy-tail regime; alpha < 2 across all studied models.
consequences: Statistical justification that SEs are an inherent (not noise) phenomenon; supports threshold robustness in Table 9.

label: Figure 12
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B alpha=1.50, x_min=74.00 ; DeepSeek-V2-Lite alpha=1.57, x_min=140.00 ; Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 alpha=1.68, x_min=300.00
paraphrase: Fitted power-law tail parameters: alpha=1.50/x_min=74 (Qwen3-30B-A3B), alpha=1.57/x_min=140 (DeepSeek-V2-Lite), alpha=1.68/x_min=300 (Mixtral-8x7B). All alpha<2 (heavy-tailed).
preconditions: MLE power-law fit on C4 calibration.
consequences: Numerical evidence that SE tail is statistically robust; x_min coincides with the (1/10)*amax cutoff used in Eq.6.

label: Table 9
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B: 3 SEs identified for every combination of percentile in {P95,P99,P99.5,P99.9} and amax-fraction in {0.07,0.08,0.09,0.10}, on both C4 and WikiText-2; DeepSeek-V2-Lite: 2; Mixtral-8x7B: 1
paraphrase: SE counts are insensitive to threshold choice: any (P95..P99.9) AND (0.07..0.10)*amax combo on C4 or WikiText-2 yields the same SE counts (3/2/1) on Qwen3/DSV2-Lite/Mixtral.
preconditions: Thresholds in the stated ranges.
consequences: Spec can pick any reasonable threshold inside this band and obtain the canonical SE set; (P99.5, 0.1*amax) is the canonical choice in Eq.6 but not load-bearing.

label: Table 10
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base / Qwen3-30B-A3B / Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 / Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507: Layer 1 Expert 68, Layer 2 Expert 92, Layer 3 Expert 82
paraphrase: All four Qwen3-30B-A3B checkpoints (Base, post-trained, Instruct-2507, Thinking-2507) share the identical SE set {(L1,E68),(L2,E92),(L3,E82)}.
preconditions: Eq.6 applied independently to each checkpoint with C4 calibration.
consequences: Strong invariance across post-training stages; SE set transfers between Qwen3-30B-A3B variants.

label: Alg. 1 input/output
verbatim: Input: Model with E experts per layer, calibration dataset D ; Output: Set of Super Experts S
paraphrase: Algorithm 1 (Calibration-based Super Experts Profiling) consumes a per-layer-E-expert MoE model plus a calibration dataset D and emits a set S of (layer, expert) SE indices.
preconditions: MoE LLM with uniform per-layer expert count E; calibration corpus D.
consequences: Defines the canonical Stage-1 contract that any reference implementation must match.

label: Alg. 1 Stage 1
verbatim: Stage 1: Calibration of MA-formation Layers. L <- empty; for each batch x in D: for each layer l: compute Hl(x); if MA pattern detected in Hl(x): L <- L union {l}
paraphrase: Stage 1 of profiling: scan each calibration batch and each decoder layer; if hidden state Hl(x) exhibits an MA pattern, add l to layer set L.
preconditions: An MA-pattern detector (e.g., max-magnitude jump between layers) must be defined.
consequences: L is the layer-set restriction in Eq.6; without it, late-layer outlier experts would be misclassified as SEs.

label: Alg. 1 Stage 2 collection
verbatim: Stage 2: Identification of Super-Experts. A <- empty; for each batch x in D: for each layer l in L: for each expert e: compute output h_{l,e}(x) before down_proj; a_{l,e} <- max_{x in D} | h_{l,e}(x) * W^{l,e}_{down_proj} |; A <- A union {a_{l,e}}
paraphrase: For each (l,e) with l in L, take the max over all calibration tokens of |h_{l,e}(x) * W_d^{l,e}| (i.e., the down_proj output magnitude); collect into A.
preconditions: Pre-down_proj activation h_{l,e} is the SwiGLU-gated hidden tensor; the max is over all tokens in all batches, not a per-batch max.
consequences: Establishes that a_{l,e} is a SCALAR max (over tokens and channels) of the down_proj output magnitude; not an L2 norm or mean.

label: Alg. 1 Stage 2 threshold
verbatim: P99.5 <- Percentile99.5(A) ; a_max <- max(A) ; S <- empty ; for each (l,e) with a_{l,e} in A: if a_{l,e} > P99.5 and a_{l,e} > (1/10) a_max: S <- S union {(l,e)} ; return S
paraphrase: After collecting all a_{l,e} into A, compute P99.5 over A and a_max over A; an expert (l,e) is added to S iff a_{l,e} exceeds BOTH P99.5 AND 0.1*a_max. Return S.
preconditions: P99.5 and a_max computed over the FULL A across layers in L (not per-layer).
consequences: Canonical implementation: thresholds are GLOBAL across L (not per-layer), which is critical to avoid false positives in narrow-tail layers.

verbatim: Extreme activation outliers from these SEs are propagated into the hidden states between decoders via residual summation, progressively leading to massive activation.
paraphrase: MAs accumulate progressively across multiple early decoder layers in MoE models (unlike dense LLMs where MAs typically arise in a single layer).
preconditions: Multi-layer SE structure (e.g., 3 SEs in 3 consecutive early layers in Qwen3-30B-A3B).
consequences: Detector must consider multiple early layers; pruning all SEs is needed to fully eliminate MAs.

label: §3.1 / Fig. 4 caption
verbatim: MAs are computed using 100 input samples from the C4 dataset, each with a length of 2K.
paraphrase: MA-detection calibration uses 100 samples of 2048-token sequences from C4.
preconditions: C4 dataset.
consequences: Sets minimum reasonable calibration size for SE/MA detection (~200K tokens).

verbatim: Unlike dense LLMs, where such behavior typically occurs within a single layer, MoE models exhibit the progressive formation of systematic outliers by SEs across multiple layers.
paraphrase: MoE LLMs spread the SE -> MA -> AS pipeline over multiple early decoder layers, while dense LLMs concentrate it in one layer.
preconditions: MoE architecture with multiple consecutive SE-bearing layers.
consequences: Justifies cross-layer iteration in Algorithm 1's Stage 1 and Stage 2.

verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite adopts a hybrid architecture, wherein the first layer utilizes dense MLPs, while the remaining layers are based on MoE blocks.
paraphrase: DeepSeek-V2-Lite layer 0 is a dense MLP (no MoE/router); layers 1+ are MoE.
preconditions: DeepSeek-V2-Lite{,-Chat}.
consequences: Stage-1 SE detector must skip layer 0 on DeepSeek-V2-Lite; SE indices like (L3,E54) are 0-indexed across MoE layers per the paper convention.

label: §3.2.2 / Appendix D
verbatim: the distribution of SEs remains highly stable, regardless of variations in the input data domain.
paraphrase: SE distribution is invariant across calibration corpora (C4, WikiText-2, C-Eval, GSM8K, HumanEval, Pile-of-Law, MedDialog).
preconditions: Cross-domain experiments in Appendices D and J.
consequences: Compliance test: re-running SE detection with a different corpus must yield the same SE set.

verbatim: They are limited in number, yet their values are orders of magnitude larger than those of other activations (e.g., up to 100,000 times larger).
paraphrase: Massive activations are up to 1e5x larger than typical hidden-state activations.
preconditions: Hidden-state magnitude survey.
consequences: Sanity-check magnitude: any candidate-SE that does NOT produce ~>=1000x activations vs other experts is suspicious.

label: §5.2 / Fig. 8
verbatim: Figures (c) and (d) illustrate the attention scores following SE pruning, where the AS completely disappears.
paraphrase: Visualization confirms attention sinks vanish in Layer 25 Head 10 and Layer 35 Head 10 of Qwen3-30B-A3B once SEs are pruned.
preconditions: Qwen3-30B-A3B; sample attention map.
consequences: Visual diagnostic for SE-protection failures.

verbatim: maintaining ASs is essential for preventing undesirable distributional shifts of attention scores.
paraphrase: Attention sinks must be preserved; their loss causes catastrophic distributional shift of attention scores.
preconditions: Sparse-attention/KV-compression literature.
consequences: Generalizes the requirement: any compression technique that destroys ASs (including SE pruning) is unsafe.

verbatim: This phenomenon typically occurs in a single layer (e.g., Mixtral) or in just a few layers (e.g., Qwen3-30B-A3B) starting from the initial decoder layers, ultimately leading to stable MAs across nearly all subsequent layers.
paraphrase: MA formation begins in the first 1-3 decoder layers (model-dependent) and stabilizes thereafter; Mixtral=1 layer, Qwen3-30B-A3B=3 layers.
preconditions: MA-formation phenomenology.
consequences: Stage 1 layer-set L is the small set of EARLY layers, not the full layer stack.

verbatim: We use the original model and results from random pruning of an equivalent number of experts as baselines. Random pruning is implemented in two ways: globally across all layers, or within the same layers as the SEs, which we refer to as layer-controlled (LC) random pruning.
paraphrase: SE-importance evaluation uses two random baselines: global random and layer-controlled (LC) random pruning at equal expert count.
preconditions: Equal cardinality |SE-set|.
consequences: Compliance reproduction must include LC-random as the strongest comparable baseline.

verbatim: random pruning has a negligible impact, underscoring the crucial role of SEs.
paraphrase: Across all three non-reasoning models, random and LC-random expert pruning produce drop-rates within ~+/-0.6% of baseline — i.e., effectively lossless.
preconditions: Same expert count as SE set; results averaged over 5 runs.
consequences: Confirms that the catastrophic drop is SE-specific, not a generic 'one expert removed' effect.

label: Abstract / §4.2
verbatim: prune just three out of 6,144
paraphrase: Qwen3-30B-A3B has 48 layers x 128 experts = 6144 expert slots, of which exactly 3 are SEs.
preconditions: Qwen3-30B-A3B architecture.
consequences: The SE-set cardinality is integer-exact; any detector outputting !=3 SEs on Qwen3-30B-A3B is a regression.

label: Table 1
verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 ... SE Count 10
paraphrase: DeepSeek-R1 has exactly 10 SEs.
preconditions: Eq.6 applied to DeepSeek-R1 on C4.
consequences: Detector must produce 10 SEs on DeepSeek-R1; deviation is a regression.

label: Table 1 / §3.2.2
verbatim: SEs are consistently present across the investigated models, accounting for less than 0.5% of all experts.
paraphrase: Across all evaluated MoE LLMs, SE proportion is <0.5% of all expert slots.
preconditions: Per-model SE counts: Qwen3 0.05%, DeepSeek-R1 0.06%, DSV2-Lite 0.11%, Mixtral 0.39%.
consequences: Sanity bound: any detector returning >0.5% of experts as SEs is misconfigured.

verbatim: we compute the maximum output magnitudes to the down_proj for all experts across all layers
paraphrase: The detection signal is the per-expert MAXIMUM (over tokens) of the down_proj OUTPUT magnitude, computed for every expert in every layer initially, then filtered to l in L.
preconditions: down_proj output (post-W_d, before adding to residual).
consequences: Implementation must hook on the down_proj OUTPUT, not its input or weight statistics.

verbatim: We have developed an automated tool for rapid and precise SE profiling based on this definition. The code is provided in https://github.com/ZunhaiSu/Super-Experts-Profilling.
paraphrase: Reference implementation is open-sourced at https://github.com/ZunhaiSu/Super-Experts-Profilling.
preconditions: Internet access.
consequences: Spec implementations may cross-check against this reference.

label: §5.1 / Appendix F
verbatim: this behavior is independent of the input dataset (see Appendix F), which also explains why SEs distributions are model-specific.
paraphrase: The router-score-on-sink-token >> router-score-on-non-sink-token pattern for SEs is invariant across calibration corpora; SE identity is therefore an intrinsic property of model weights.
preconditions: Cross-corpus router-score visualization.
consequences: Confirms SE detection is data-agnostic and weight-determined.

label: Appendix H / Table 8
verbatim: removing the SWs consistently leads to repetitive and uninformative outputs across all models, demonstrating that these weights are critical contributors to SE importance.
paraphrase: Within an SE, a tiny number of 'super weights' (SWs) in down_proj account for SE behavior; pruning the SW alone reproduces the SE-pruning failure mode.
preconditions: SW = 1-4 entries of W_d as listed in Table 8.
consequences: Quantization of SE down_proj must protect at minimum these SW rows/cols (e.g., row 940 in Qwen3-30B-A3B SE down_projs).

label: Appendix H
verbatim: in dense LLMs, the single FFN layer exhibiting massive activations can be seen as playing a role analogous to that of an SE.
paraphrase: Dense LLMs do not have SEs per se, but the single FFN layer where MAs emerge plays the same functional role.
preconditions: Dense LLMs (e.g., Llama-3.2-1B, LLaVA-V1.5-7B).
consequences: Concept of SE generalizes; spec applies to MoE only but the underlying outlier-mechanism is universal.

label: Appendix H / Fig. 23
verbatim: the top features associated with the SWs neuron consistently exhibit pronounced activation at the end of text token
paraphrase: SAE-decoded top SW-correlated features fire on end-of-text tokens, explaining why SE/SW pruning yields runaway repetitive generation.
preconditions: SAE trained on Llama-3.2-1B Layer-1 FFN outputs (C4).
consequences: Mechanistic explanation for the 'never emits EOS' behavior under SE pruning.

label: Table 8
paraphrase: Qwen3-30B-A3B has 48 decoder layers (indexed 0-47); each expert's down_proj weight has shape (2048, 768) (intermediate hidden -> model hidden).
preconditions: Qwen3-30B-A3B architecture.
consequences: Allows compliance reproduction to size buffers correctly.

label: Figure 12 / Appendix I
verbatim: the estimated tail exponent α is below 2, placing the distribution within the heavy-tailed regime.
paraphrase: Across all studied MoE LLMs, the power-law fit to the CCDF of A has alpha < 2 (heavy-tailed; infinite variance).
preconditions: MLE power-law fit on C4 calibration.
consequences: Statistical certainty that SEs are intrinsic, not noise — supports threshold robustness.

label: §5.1 / Fig. 7
verbatim: The sink token subsequently produces activation outliers in the output through weight outliers in the down_proj. Through the residual connections, these outliers propagate into the hidden states as MAs. At the attention layers, such tokens then attract disproportionate attention and ultimately emerge as attention sinks.
paraphrase: Mechanism: (1) router upweights SE on sink token, (2) SE's down_proj weight outliers convert sink-token activation into output outlier, (3) residual carries outlier into hidden state as MA, (4) MA-bearing token attracts attention -> AS.
preconditions: MoE LLM with sink-token-attracted attention pattern.
consequences: End-to-end mechanism that any SE-protection scheme must preserve at every step.

label: Appendix J / Fig. 24-29
verbatim: the distribution of SEs remains highly consistent across these varied domains and OoD datasets. Moreover, the sink tokens consistently appear as the first token.
paraphrase: Across C4, C-Eval, GSM8K, HumanEval, Pile-of-Law, and MedDialog, the SE distribution and sink-token-is-first-token pattern hold uniformly.
preconditions: Six diverse calibration corpora including OoD legal/medical text.
consequences: Calibration-corpus choice has no effect on SE detection outcomes.

label: §9 Reproducibility
verbatim: All models, datasets, experimental setups, and hyperparameters used in this work are thoroughly documented. Key code components are provided in the supplementary materials, and the full algorithmic procedures are detailed in the appendix.
paraphrase: Authors assert full reproducibility via documented setups and supplementary code.
preconditions: Access to the GitHub repository and the appendix.
consequences: Compliance audit may consult the GitHub reference for detailed implementation choices when paper is ambiguous.
